zh.lifeSummary: 當Daniel I van der Merwede於1125年3月10日出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特斯登時，他的父親Herman van Heusden54歲，母親Christina Arentsbergh29歲。他於1154年與Justine van Heeze-Millen在德國海藍-普法爾茲道恩薩爾姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1184年在荷蘭布拉班特斯登被列為heer van heusden。他去世於1187年2月2日在他的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Daniel I van der Merwede于1125年3月10日出生在荷兰努-布拉班特斯登时，他的父亲Herman van Heusden54岁，母亲Christina Arentsbergh29岁。他于1154年与Justine van Heeze-Millen在德国海藍-普法爾茲道恩薩爾姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1184年在荷兰布拉班特斯登被列为heer van heusden。他去世于1187年2月2日在他的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Daniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘God is my judge’ in Hebrew), borne by the prophet whose story is told in the Book of Daniel. He was an Israelite slave of the Assyrian king Nebuchadnezzar, who obtained great favour through his skill in interpreting dreams and the ‘writing on the wall’ at the feast held by Nebuchadnezzar's son Belshazzar. His enemies managed to get him cast into a lions' den, but he was saved by God. This was a favourite tale in the Middle Ages, often represented in miracle plays. The name has been perennially popular among English speakers since the 16th century and has been particularly favoured since the 1980s.

en.lifeSummary: When Daniel I van der Merwede was born on 10 March 1125, in Heusden, North Brabant, Netherlands, his father, Herman van Heusden, was 54 and his mother, Christina Arentsbergh, was 29. He married Justine van Heeze-Millen in 1154, in Salm, Daun, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1184, at the age of 59, his occupation is listed as heer van heusden in Heusden, Brabant, Netherlands. He died on 2 February 1187, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Daniel I van der Merwede est né le 10 mars 1125, à Heusden, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Herman van Heusden, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Christina Arentsbergh, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Justine van Heeze-Millen en 1154, à Salm, Daun, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1184, à l’âge de 59 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est heer van heusden à Heusden, Brabant, Pays-Bas. Il est décédé le 2 février 1187, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Daniel I van der Merwede am 10. März 1125 in Heusden, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herman van Heusden 54 und seine Mutter Christina Arentsbergh 29. Er hat 1154 in Salm, Daun, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland Justine van Heeze-Millen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1184, als er 59 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als heer van heusden in Heusden, Brabant, Niederlande aufgeführt. Er starb am 2. Februar 1187 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Daniel I van der Merwede è nato il il 10 marzo 1125 nel Heusden, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Herman van Heusden, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Christina Arentsbergh, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Justine van Heeze-Millen nel 1154, nel Salm, Daun, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1184, all'età di 59 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come heer van heusden nel Heusden, Brabant, Paesi Bassi. Morì il 2 febbraio 1187, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Daniel I van der Merwedeが1125年3月10日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントヘウスデンで生まれた時，父Herman van Heusdenは54歳，母Christina Arentsberghは29歳であった。1154年にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズダウンサルムでJustine van Heeze-Millenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1184年，59歳の時，オランダブラバントヘウスデンでの職業はheer van heusdenと記録されている。彼は1187年2月2日に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Daniel I van der Merwede(이)가 1125년 3월 10일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 헤우즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herman van Heusden(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina Arentsbergh(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1154년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 도 살름에서 Justine van Heeze-Millen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1184년, 59세의 나이로 그의 직업은 네덜란드 브라반트 헤우즈든에서 heer van heusden이었다. 그는 1187년 2월 2일 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Heusden, North Brabant, Netherlands: Heusden, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Daniel I van der Merwede nasceu em 10 de março de 1125, em Heusden, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Herman van Heusden, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Christina Arentsbergh, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Justine van Heeze-Millen em 1154, em Salm, Daun, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1184, com 59 anos, sua profissão aparece como heer van heusden em Heusden, Brabant, Países Baixos. Ele faleceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1187, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Daniel I van der Merwede родился 10 марта 1125 г. в местности хеусден, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Herman van Heusden было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Christina Arentsbergh, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Justine van Heeze-Millen в 1154 г. в местности салм, даун, раинланд-пфалз, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1184 году, в возрасте 59 лет,  он работал по профессии heer van heusden в местности хеусден, брабант, Нидерланды. Он умер 2 февраля 1187 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Daniel I van der Merwede nació el 10 de marzo de 1125, en Heusden, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, su papá, Herman van Heusden, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Christina Arentsbergh, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Justine van Heeze-Millen en 1154, en Salm, Daun, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1184, a la edad de 59 años, su ocupación registrada era heer van heusden en Heusden, Brabant, Países Bajos. Murió el 2 de febrero de 1187, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
